channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: White House says health insurance needs to fully cover condoms, other over-the-counter birth control
item.description: People with private health insurance would be able to pick up over-the-counter methods like condoms, the “morning after” pill and birth control pills for free under a new rule the White House proposed on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea calls for immediate withdrawal of North Korean troops allegedly in Russia
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea on Monday demanded the immediate pullout of North Korean troops allegedly deployed in Russia as it summoned the Russian ambassador to protest deepening military cooperation between Pyongyang and Moscow.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Georgia islanders rushed to rescue survivors after dock walkway collapse that killed 7
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Orange life jackets bobbed in the water, where bystanders rushed to form a human chain for passing survivors to safety. Others shouldered the task of wrapping dead bodies in blankets and carrying them to shore.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: President Biden and first lady toast a reimagined White House tour that she's unveiling on Monday
item.description: President Joe Biden and first lady Jill Biden on Sunday toasted the unveiling of a long-awaited reimagined public White House tour.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans head to court in expanding battle about overseas ballots
item.description: Overseas voting has become the latest battlefront in Republicans’ legal challenges leading up to the election.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Mormon church now offering sleeveless garments for members in warmer climates
item.description: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints is now offering sleeveless garments for members in hot and humid climates, according to a Church spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 Navy aviators declared dead after fighter jet crash in Washington state
item.description: The aviators' names won’t be released until a day after their next of kin have been notified, the Navy said in a statement Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One of the last Navajo Code Talkers from World War II dies at 107
item.description: WINDOW ROCK, Ariz. (AP) — John Kinsel Sr., one of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II based on the tribe's native language, has died. He was 107. Navajo Nation officials in Window Rock announced Kinsel’s death on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida restaurant burns down on day it reopened after hurricane
item.description: As employees were closing the Boathouse Tiki Bar and Grill, a fire broke out, according to the Cape Coral Fire Department.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, NewsNation

item.title: Driving the vote: New Jersey GOP tries to flip state to red
item.description: New Jersey hasn't gone red since 1988 — but that isn't stopping GOP volunteers from making their voices heard.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Popular Florida restaurant burns down on day it reopened after hurricane
item.description: A popular restaurant in Cape Coral burned down on the same day it had reopened after Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In New York, a constitutional amendment provides election fodder for the left and the right
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Democrats pushed to get a constitutional amendment on New York's ballot because they believed it could energize liberals eager to protect abortion rights.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says he'll award $1M every day to Pennsylvania voters who sign his petition
item.description: Elon Musk announced he will pay up to $1 million per day to Pennsylvania voters who sign his America PAC petition leading up to Election Day. The petition asks signers to support free speech and the right to bear arms.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame turns up starpower to induct new members: Who made it in
item.description: It was a five-hour-plus show on Saturday that helped induct the newest members of the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Many gift-buying parents pressured to overspend this holiday season: Survey
item.description: (NerdWallet) - Holiday shopping season is upon us, and the pressure is on, or at least it feels that way for some parents.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, KTLA 5

item.title: Indiana 3-year-old scratched by monkey inside Walmart
item.description: BEDFORD, Ind. (WXIN) — A 3-year-old is recovering after being scratched this week by a monkey inside a central Indiana Walmart store. The incident reportedly occurred on Wednesday afternoon inside the Walmart Supercenter in Bedford, Indiana. Local police responded to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman needing double-lung transplant marries her donor's brother
item.description: One of the scariest moments turned into something more beautiful than one woman could've ever imagined.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris's and Trump's most likely paths to victory in battleground states
item.description: Vice President Harris and former President Trump both have multiple paths to victory in their bids to get 270 electoral votes. The candidates are zeroing in on the seven battleground states that will most likely decide which candidate will win the presidency in November.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Texas man arrested for allegedly stealing neighbors' dogs, killing them
item.description: Deputies went to the home of Stuart Duncan Hammonds, 24, where they found skeletal remains of several animals. Many had head trauma like they had been shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana 3-year-old scratched by monkey inside Walmart
item.description: BEDFORD, Ind. (WXIN) — A 3-year-old is recovering after being scratched by a monkey inside a central Indiana Walmart store this week. The incident reportedly occurred on Wednesday afternoon inside the Walmart Supercenter in Bedford, Indiana. Local police responded to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, ABC 4

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play high school football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, WGN 9

item.title: Why experts say keeping standard time is 'undeniably' better for us
item.description: Surveys have found that most Americans want to do away with the twice-a-year changing of the clocks. But whether we should observe daylight saving or standard time year round will depend on who you ask.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Mississippi couple celebrates 60 years of marriage at Chick-Fil-A
item.description: A Mississippi couple celebrated their 60th marriage anniversary at Chick-fil-A after eating lunch at the restaurant every day for the past two years.
item.dc:creator: Shay Simon, KRON4

item.title: Where is the 'best restroom' in the U.S.? In a Utah gas station
item.description: The runners-up of this year's contest included a disco party bathroom, a bathroom full of mirrors and neon lights, and a very reflective 80s-inspired restroom in a brunch restaurant, among others.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KRON4

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: With brain injuries a growing problem, the US military tests how to protect troops from blasts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The blast shook the ground and its red flash of fire covered the doorway as U.S. special operations forces blew open a door during a recent training exercise.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NOAA releases 2024-25 winter weather outlook: Here's what to expect
item.description: Depending on where you live in the U.S., this winter could bring a mix of snow and rain or unseasonably warm weather, according to climate experts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No. 2 Oregon routs Purdue 35-0, remains on top of Big Ten with first road shutout in over 30 years
item.description: WEST LAFAYETTE, Ind. (AP) — Oregon coach Dan Lanning challenged his team to see how it would respond to a long road trip on a short week following its 32-31 victory over then-No. 2 Ohio State last week. The No. 2 Ducks aced that test Friday night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lynx force Game 5 of WNBA Finals on Carleton's free throws to beat Liberty 82-80
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — With their season on the line, the Minnesota Lynx did just enough to stave off elimination and force a decisive Game 5 of the WNBA Finals. Bridget Carleton made two free throws with 2 seconds left, and the Lynx beat the New York Liberty 82-80 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black male teachers are a rarity in preschools. This pioneering program wants to change that
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Before 19-year-old Davontez Johnson found himself in a preschool classroom at Dorothy I. Heights Elementary, he was a senior at a nearby high school who, like many students his age, was unsure of what he wanted to do with his life.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yankees get to Guardians closer Clase again, score 2 runs in 9th to win 8-6 and take 3-1 ALCS lead
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Cleveland All-Star closer Emmanuel Clase gave up two runs in the ninth inning and the New York Yankees moved closer to another World Series, beating the Guardians 8-6 on Friday night to take a 3-1 lead in the American League Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Iowa school shooter ‘likely displayed warning signs’ before January attack, report finds
item.description: The shooter responsible for two deaths and multiple injuries at an Iowa high school earlier this year “likely displayed warning signs” according to a detailed report from state officials released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: “I’ll tell you what I love, I love cows but if we go with Kamala you won’t have any cows any more,” the former president responded to the child's question. “I don’t want to ruin this kid's day.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane fallout threatens to hinder voting
item.description: The fallout from a pair of deadly hurricanes threatens to roil voting in the Southeast with complications for election officials and residents alike in states key to this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC
